summary: Comprehensive backend (pytest, 15/15 passing) + frontend (Playwright mobile viewport) testing of the Silent Signal Expo+FastAPI mobile messenger. All seeded data (alex/maya/jordan, Privacy Circle group with invite circle2026) verified. Auth (register/login/me/401s/duplicate), friends list+search, rooms (list/get/create/join), messages (list/send/ephemeral), and profile update all pass. Frontend landing -> login -> chats -> chat (send + decrypt) -> ephemeral toggle -> friends -> profile flows all work end-to-end.

minor.issue: User input is passed directly into MongoDB regex without escaping ($regex with q). Potential ReDoS / regex injection. Not exploitable for data leak but could DoS. Suggest re.escape(q) before building the regex.
No brute-force lockout after N failed attempts (auth_testing playbook recommends 5-fail lockout). Demo-grade only.

Cookie is set with samesite='none' & secure=True but app runs CORS with allow_credentials=False, so cookie is essentially unused. Frontend correctly relies on Bearer token from JSON body. Not a bug, just dead-weight surface.

issues: Old test-suite messages with non-real ciphertext show as '[unable to decrypt]' bubbles. Real UI-sent messages decrypt correctly. This is test-data artifact, not a product bug — main agent may want to add a maintenance script to purge these or surface a friendlier 'message can't be decrypted on this device' state.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. RN/web shim warning, non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Escape user input before $regex in /api/friends/search to prevent regex DoS: q_escaped = re.escape(q).
(Optional) Add brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login if the playbook compliance is desired.
(Cosmetic) Suppress or fix the 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' warning by migrating to style.pointerEvents (likely from a 3rd-party RN-web component — safe to ignore for demo).
(Cosmetic) Consider purging orphaned test ciphertext rows in Privacy Circle, or render a friendlier UI for messages that fail decryption.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~650 lines; manageable now but consider splitting auth/friends/rooms/messages into separate routers as it grows.
All MongoDB ObjectIds are correctly stringified in responses (no _id leaks).
JWT secret pulled from env, bcrypt hashes used correctly ($2b$ format), Pydantic validation on every endpoint.
All interactive elements have testID attributes — good test coverage hooks.
Encryption is intentionally demo-grade (shared client passphrase) per problem statement; documented in lib/encryption.ts.
seed_data_creation: Tests created a few TEST_-prefixed users (registration tests) and one TEST_-prefixed group room. They also POST'd a couple of placeholder ciphertext messages into the seeded 'Privacy Circle' room which now show as '[unable to decrypt]' bubbles — these can be ignored or cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at https://bug-squash-30.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Demo creds alex@silent.app/demo1234, group invite circle2026. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_silent_signal.py covers auth/friends/rooms/messages/profile. Note the test_join_with_invite test increases Privacy Circle member count on each run (now 4+); harmless but expected drift.
rca_of_issue: No bugs to RCA — all targeted features work as specified.
